steps.title: Match possession and number
steps.content: The blank describes stories that belong to other people. Since people is already plural, the possessive form is people's. The context also calls for more than one story, so the noun after it needs to be plural.

steps.title: Check the noun forms
steps.content: B uses peoples without the possessive apostrophe and story's as a possessive singular. C has the plural noun stories but still lacks the possessive form people's. D has the correct possessive people's but uses story's instead of the plural stories. The remaining wording gives both the plural possessive noun and the plural noun. The answer is A.
